compute.repo.cap.AUDIT_WRITE: Write records to kernel auditing log
compute.repo.cap.BLOCK_SUSPEND: Employ features that can block system suspend (epoll EPOLLWAKEUP, etc.)
compute.repo.cap.CHOWN: Make arbitrary changes to file UIDs and GIDs
compute.repo.cap.DAC_OVERRIDE: Bypass file read, write, and execute permission checks
compute.repo.cap.FOWNER: Bypass permission checks requiring process UID to match file UID (e.g. chmod, utime)
compute.repo.cap.KILL: Bypass permission checks for sending signals
compute.repo.cap.NET_ADMIN: Perform network operations (interface config, IP firewall, masquerading, routing tables)
compute.repo.cap.NET_BIND_SERVICE: Bind a socket to privileged ports (port numbers less than 1024)
compute.repo.cap.NET_RAW: Use RAW and PACKET sockets; bind to any address for transparent proxying
compute.repo.cap.SETGID: Make arbitrary manipulations of process GIDs and supplementary GID list
compute.repo.cap.SETPCAP: Add any capability from bounding set to inheritable set; drop capabilities from bounding set
compute.repo.cap.SETUID: Make arbitrary manipulations of process UIDs (setuid, setreuid, setresuid, etc.)
compute.repo.cap.SYS_ADMIN: Perform system administration operations (mount, umount, pivot_root, swapon, etc.)
compute.repo.cap.SYS_CHROOT: Use chroot; change mount namespaces using setns
compute.repo.cap.SYS_NICE: Lower process nice value, set nice for arbitrary processes, set real-time scheduling policies
compute.repo.cap.SYS_PTRACE: Trace arbitrary processes using ptrace; inspect sensitive /proc info of other processes
compute.repo.cap.SYS_RESOURCE: Use reserved space on ext2; override disk quota limits; increase resource limits
compute.repo.cap.SYSLOG: Perform privileged syslog operations; view kernel addresses when kptr_restrict is set
compute.repo.cap.WAKE_ALARM: Set CLOCK_REALTIME_ALARM and CLOCK_BOOTTIME_ALARM timers to wake the system
compute.repo.rootfs_persistent.tips: Data will be preserved after container restarts when enabled
compute.repo.label: label
compute.repo.helper.change_config: Only the shutdown container host can adjust the configuration
compute.repo.helper.modify: Only exited containers can be modified
compute.repo.helper.terminal: Only the running or probing {0} can enter the terminal
compute.repo.helper.set_duration.alert: After expiration, the container host of the corresponding platform will be deleted. There is a certain discrepancy between the actual release time and the set time. The specific release time shall prevail.
compute.container.port_mapping.tooltip: Currently, this operation is only supported when creating a container host
compute.repo.helper.view_log: Only the running or exited {0} can enter the terminal
compute.container.monitor.pod_netio_bps_: Number of bytes sent per second
compute.container.monitor.pod_netio_pps_: Number of network packets received per second
Number of network packets per second
